content:
✔ How Purge Failures Work:
• If a purge attempt is denied, the player receives a permanent debuff.
• If the purge attempt was blocked by another player, the initiator must “pay a debt” to that player.
• If no sacrifice was found, the initiator suffers “The Curse of Incompletion” (a lingering penalty).

✔ Example Purge Failure Penalties:
• The Curse of Incompletion → The player’s next fate roll is automatically weakened.
• Debt Owed → The blocked player can demand one favor later in the semester.
• Failed Transformation → The player suffers a hybrid effect (ex: stuck between archetypes, gaining negatives from both).

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Ensures that purges are serious, irreversible attempts.
✔ Prevents players from casually trying to escape their archetype without risk.
✔ Adds weight to faction-based bargaining—players must truly commit to a purge.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 547 (chunk 547/555)
